channel.title: Numpy on Abdulkadir Güngör
channel.description: Recent content in Numpy on Abdulkadir Güngör

item.title: Software Development with Python — A Comprehensive Technical Guide from Beginner to Expert
item.description: Python, published by Guido van Rossum in 1991, has become one of the most widely used programming languages in the world today. It consistently ranks in the top three on the TIOBE index; it is the de facto industry standard in diverse fields such as data science, web development, automation, and artificial intelligence. It is appropriate to start with the syntax that provides this popularity: Python has a readable structure close to English. However, this simplicity does not hide the power underlying the language. Integration with C extensions, support for asynchronous programming, an extensive standard library, and a massive ecosystem—all of these make Python a truly multi-layered language.

item.title: Mathematical Architecture of Complex Circuits and Nodal Analysis Method
item.description: The analysis of electrical circuits is an interdisciplinary process where fundamental physical laws are blended with linear algebra. In modern engineering applications, especially in integrated circuit design and power systems simulation, it is necessary to adopt a systematic approach rather than examining the circuit on a component-by-component basis. At this point, Nodal Analysis, built upon Kirchhoff’s Current Law (KCL), allows us to reach the solution set by reducing unknown voltages in the circuit to a systematic matrix form.
